rulings.Case Title: DANNY BLACK ET AL VS. ALLIS-CHALMERS CORPORATION PRODUCT LIABILITY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 7, 2019 in Department 503, Line 1. Plaintiffs' motion for summary adjudication is denied. Plaintiffs failed to sustain their burden of demonstrating that Defendant Armstrong International Inc. does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence as to each one of its affirmative defenses. (See Decl. Mirzayan, Ex. A at p. 16, Ex. B at p. 54-56, Ex. C at p. 21, Ex. D at p. 62-63, Ex. E, Ex. F.) The burden of production "should not shift without stringent review of the direct, circumstantial and inferential evidence." (Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Construction Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 83.) Objections raised during discovery "are not the equivalent of a factually devoid responses, and therefore cannot be used to shift the burden . . .." (Gaggero v. Yura (2003) 108 Cal.App.4th 884, 893.) Even if Defendant Armstrong International Inc.'s discovery responses are factually devoid, Plaintiffs did not conduct comprehensive discovery. A party cannot rely on factually devoid discovery responses in the absence of conducting comprehensive discovery. (Scheiding, supra, 69 Cal.App.4th at 83; Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 854-855.) The Court "can infer nothing at all with respect to questions which were neither asked nor answered." (Scheiding, supra, 69 Cal.App.4th at 81.) The Court notes that Plaintiffs failed to comply with California Rules of Court, rule 3.1350(h) in preparing their separate statement of undisputed material facts. Moreover, pursuant to SF Local Rule 2.7B, the Court did not receive courtesy copies of Plaintiffs' reply papers and thus Plaintiffs' reply was not considered. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: DANNY BLACK ET AL VS. ALLIS-CHALMERS CORPORATION PRODUCT LIABILITY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 7, 2019 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant ConocoPhillips Co.'s motion for summary judgment, or in the alternative, summary adjudication is denied. Considering all evidence and inferences in favor of Plaintiffs, Defendant ConocoPhillips failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence regarding Plaintiffs' cause of action for premises liability, Plaintiffs' cause of action for loss of consortium, and Plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages. (CCP § 437c(p)(2); Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 843, 850-51.) The burden of production "should not shift without stringent review of the direct, circumstantial and inferential evidence." (Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Construction Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 83.) Defendant cannot rely on Plaintiffs' factually devoid discovery responses in the absence of conducting comprehensive discovery. (Id. at 83; Aguilar, supra, 25 Cal.4th at 854-855.) Defendant's motion for summary adjudication as to Plaintiffs' cause of action for premises liability is denied. "[W]hen the landowner knows or should know of a concealed hazard on its premises, then under ordinary premises liability principles, the landowner may be liable for a resultant injury to those employees." (Kinsmann v. Unocal Corp. (2005) 37 Cal.4th 659, 674.) Mr. Black testified that he worked at the Tosco refinery near Martinez, CA for six to eight week, working 8-16 hour shifts, 5 days a week. (Decl. Savage, Ex. C, Depo. Black at 990:8-15, 990:22-991:23, 991:25-992:1.) Mr. Black was an employee of Oscar Erickson and not Tesco Co. (Id. at 996:10-997:3.) Mr. Black stated that while working at the Tosco refinery he performed clean up work, he worked around Tosco employees scraping gaskets, he removed gaskets himself, and that he removed asbestos thermal insulation. (Id. at 1002:1-1005:8-17.) Defendant ConocoPhillips failed to shift burden because (1) ConocoPhillips failed to show that it did not know or reasonably should have known of the pre-existing hazardous condition at the Tosco refinery; (2) ConocoPhilips failed to demonstrate that Oscar Erickson knew or could reasonably ascertain the condition; and (3) ConocoPhillips failed to present any evidence that it warned Oscar Erickson of the hazard. (See Kinsmann v. Unocal Corp., supra, 37 Cal.4th at 675.) Even if Defendant ConocoPhillips shifted burden, the declarations of Mr. Black and Charles Ay create triable issues of material fact as to whether Mr. Black was exposed to asbestos while working at the Tosco refinery. (See Decl. Mirzayan, Ex. B, Ex. C.) Because Plaintiffs' cause of action for premises liability survives summary adjudication, Defendant ConocoPhillips' summary adjudication as to Plaintiffs' cause of action for loss of consortium is denied. (See Vanhooser v. Superior Court (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 921, 927; see Leonard v. John Crane, Inc. (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 1274, 1290.) Summary judgment must "be granted if all the papers submitted show that there is no triable issue as to any material fact and that the moving party is entitled to judgment as a matter of law." (CCP § 437c(c).) Defendant ConocoPhillips' motion for summary judgment is denied because Plaintiffs' causes of action for premise liability and loss of consortium survive summary adjudication. (Tentative ruling continued, see next entry)

rulings.Case Title: DANNY BLACK ET AL VS. ALLIS-CHALMERS CORPORATION PRODUCT LIABILITY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Conocophillips Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only.)
rulings.Rulings: (PART 2 OF 2) (Tentative ruling continued from previous entry) Defendant ConocoPhillips' motion for summary adjudication as to Plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant ConocoPhillips failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain clear and convincing evidence that Defendant ConocoPhillips acted with the requisite malice, fraud, or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. (See Decl. Savage, Ex. A-1 at p. 9, Ex. A-2 at p. 7, Ex. A-3 at p.4, Ex. B-1 at 34-41, Ex. B-2 at p. 22-24, Ex. B at p. 5.) The burden of production "should not shift without stringent review of the direct, circumstantial and inferential evidence." (Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Construction Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 83.) Objections raised during discovery "are not the equivalent of a factually devoid responses, and therefore cannot be used to shift the burden from the defendant to the plaintiff under Code of Civil Procedure section 437c, subdivision (p)(2)." (Gaggero v. Yura (2003) 108 Cal.App.4th 884, 893.) Even if Plaintiffs' discovery responses are factually devoid, Defendant ConocoPhillips did not conduct comprehensive discovery as to punitive damages. Defendant cannot rely on Plaintiffs' factually devoid discovery responses in the absence of conducting comprehensive discovery. (Scheiding, supra, 69 Cal.App.4th at 83; Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 854-855.) The Court "can infer nothing at all with respect to questions which were neither asked nor answered." (See Scheiding, supra, 69 Cal.App.4th at 81.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of a reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/CML)
